frameworks.name: hierarchical / multi-level encoding
frameworks.one_line: memory represents items jointly with group and display-level statistics

frameworks.name: ensemble statistics / summary representation
frameworks.one_line: mean size of a set biases memory of individual items

frameworks.name: independent-item visual working memory models
frameworks.one_line: items stored as independent units without between-item interactions

frameworks.name: constructive memory
frameworks.one_line: memory integrates information across levels of abstraction

core_question: Does visual working memory encode items independently, or does it integrate items with higher-order ensemble statistics of the display?
key_assumptions: real-world displays carry higher-order structure beyond single items
observers can compute summary statistics such as mean size
memory can be probed for individual-item size while ensemble context is present
color provides a grouping cue that defines sub-ensembles
manipulations: set-level mean size of items sharing a color
display-level mean size across all items
grouping of items by color into sub-ensembles
key_findings: remembered size of each item is biased toward the mean size of same-color items
remembered size is also biased toward the mean size of all items in the display
biases operate simultaneously at multiple grouping levels
interpretations: visual working memory is constructive, encoding displays at multiple levels of abstraction
memory integrates across item, group, and ensemble levels rather than storing veridical independent items
